skill.name: nuxt
skill.description: Guidance for building and maintaining Nuxt applications with SSR, file-based routing, data fetching, modules, server routes, and deployment patterns.
skill.summary: Use Nuxt framework guidance for SSR, routing, data fetching, modules, and deployment decisions.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, script, and environment patterns, but review shows they are Markdown documentation examples for Nuxt. No prompt injection, hidden executable code, credential exfiltration, or malicious automation was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Markdown command examples flagged as execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The cited locations are Markdown examples showing Nuxt CLI usage or generation metadata, not executable scripts run by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown references and the command text is inside documentation blocks. No executable wrapper, hook, or script file was found at these locations.

low_findings.title: Network and secret terms are framework documentation
low_findings.description: Network, cookie, and environment findings are false positives for marketplace security. The examples explain Nuxt useFetch, runtimeConfig, HTTP cookies, and server-side token verification rather than exfiltrating data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed snippets are instructional Nuxt code samples. They do not contain a destination that receives secrets, and no skill runtime code performs network requests.

low_findings.title: Filesystem indicators are Nuxt project conventions
low_findings.description: Filesystem findings are false positives. The cited paths describe Nuxt layers, generated output folders, and special project files such as .env, not arbitrary file reads or writes by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal and hidden-file matches are literal documentation of accepted Nuxt paths. There is no code path that uses user input to access these files.

content.user_title: Build Nuxt Apps With Framework Guidance
content.value_statement: Nuxt projects require correct choices across SSR, routing, data fetching, modules, and deployment. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Nuxt guidance from framework-oriented references.
actual_capabilities: Explains Nuxt directory structure, special files, and project organization.
Guides routing, layouts, middleware, route validation, and navigation patterns.
Supports SSR-safe data fetching with useFetch, useAsyncData, and $fetch guidance.
Covers Nuxt server routes, middleware, cookies, headers, and Nitro deployment basics.
Provides module authoring, hooks, layers, auto-imports, and configuration guidance.
Helps compare rendering modes, route rules, static generation, and deployment presets.
limitations: It is based on Nuxt 3.x reference material generated on 2026-01-28.
It does not execute, test, or deploy Nuxt projects by itself.
It cannot verify private project code unless the user provides the code or repository context.
It may not include changes introduced after the referenced Nuxt documentation snapshot.

use_cases.title: Plan a Nuxt application structure
use_cases.description: Choose folders, routing conventions, middleware placement, and configuration boundaries for a new Nuxt project.

use_cases.title: Fix SSR and hydration issues
use_cases.description: Identify unsafe browser APIs, shared server state, and data fetching patterns that cause hydration mismatches.

use_cases.title: Prepare Nuxt for deployment
use_cases.description: Select rendering modes, Nitro presets, static generation, and runtime configuration for a target hosting platform.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Nuxt concept
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how Nuxt file-based routing works for this app. Include where pages, layouts, middleware, and route validation belong.

prompt_templates.title: Review data fetching
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Nuxt data fetching approach. Tell me when to use useFetch, useAsyncData, or $fetch, and point out SSR risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design server routes
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the Nuxt server route structure for this feature. Include API routes, middleware, request validation, cookies, and runtime config needs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan advanced architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Nuxt architecture plan using layers, modules, hooks, rendering rules, and deployment presets for these product requirements.

output_examples.input: Review a Nuxt page that fetches initial product data with a click handler fallback.
output: Recommendation to use useFetch for initial SSR data.
Guidance to reserve $fetch for user-triggered actions.
Notes about loading, error, refresh, and hydration behavior.

output_examples.input: Plan deployment for a Nuxt app with marketing pages and authenticated dashboard routes.
output: Suggested hybrid rendering strategy by route type.
Nitro deployment considerations for the selected platform.
Runtime configuration notes for public and server-only values.

output_examples.input: Organize shared UI and composables across multiple Nuxt applications.
output: Layer-based sharing plan for components, composables, layouts, and config.
Module authoring guidance when behavior should be packaged.
Naming and auto-import advice to avoid collisions.

best_practices: Provide the Nuxt version, deployment target, and rendering requirements before asking for architecture advice.
Include relevant files such as nuxt.config.ts, route files, composables, and server handlers for precise review.
Ask for SSR and client behavior separately when debugging hydration, state, or data fetching issues.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic Vue advice when the issue depends on Nuxt routing, Nitro, or SSR behavior.
Do not expose server-only runtime configuration under public config keys.
Do not use browser-only APIs during server rendering without a client-only guard or alternative.

faq.question: What Nuxt version does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Nuxt 3.x reference material generated on 2026-01-28.

faq.question: Can it create a complete Nuxt app?
faq.answer: It can guide structure and implementation, but it does not scaffold or run a project by itself.

faq.question: Does it cover server routes?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Nuxt server API routes, middleware, headers, cookies, and Nitro basics.

faq.question: Can it help with hydration errors?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains SSR-safe state, browser-only code, and data fetching patterns that affect hydration.

faq.question: Does it include deployment guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers static generation, Node servers, Nitro presets, route rules, and common hosting targets.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation false positives and no confirmed malicious behavior.
